name: Dark Mode Best Practices
description: Guidelines for implementing accessible and user-friendly dark mode

user_preference.description: Respect user's system preference and provide manual toggle

avoid_pure_black.reason: Causes eye strain, poor contrast on OLED

color_saturation.rationale: Bright saturated colors cause eye strain on dark backgrounds
color_saturation.adjustment: Increase lightness by 20-30%, decrease saturation by 10-20%

dark_mode_challenges: Pure white on pure black is too harsh (15:1+)
Consider slight tint to reduce harshness
Test with actual dark mode users
Some colors need more adjustment than others

css_variables.description: Use CSS custom properties for theme switching

prevent_flash.description: Prevent white flash on page load
prevent_flash.solution: Inline theme script in <head> before CSS

images.problem: Photos/images may look washed out in dark mode

solutions.name: Different images
solutions.implementation: Use picture element with media query for dark mode

shadows.note: Increase shadow opacity in dark mode for visibility

borders.alternative: Use subtle backgrounds instead of borders

icons.icon_libraries: Choose icons that work in both modes
icons.consideration: Some icons may need different styles in dark mode

testing_checklist: Test all text for sufficient contrast (4.5:1 min)
Verify images don't look washed out
Check form inputs are clearly visible
Test on OLED screens (blacks should be true black)
Verify all interactive elements are visible

common_mistakes.problem: Too harsh, poor for OLED displays
Vibrant colors cause eye strain on dark backgrounds

common_mistakes.solution: Increase opacity or use lighter shadows

common_mistakes.mistake: Flash of wrong theme on load
common_mistakes.solution: Inline script in <head> to set theme before render

accessibility: Maintain WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)
Don't rely on color alone to convey information
Ensure focus indicators are visible in both modes
Announce theme change to screen readers
